---
title: "Figure 5 — conceptual schematic of the non-Gaussian domains"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Conceptual (not data) schematic: where each source of non-Gaussianity dominates on the design plane
(x = interval Delta/tau, y = N_ch), i.e. where the Gaussian likelihood is distorted.
 - Gaussian (CLT): many channels AND many transitions per interval -> distortion -> 1 (top-right).
 - Multinomial / binomial: FEW channels -> the channel-occupancy count is not Gaussian (bottom band).
 - Poisson / telegraph: SHORT interval -> few switching events per interval, the discrete on/off
   dominates (left band). Bottom-left = both fail = worst distortion.
This is the legend of the empirical distortion heatmaps.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r plot, fig.width = 5.2, fig.height = 4.2}
XB <- log10(c(.01, .1, 1)); YB <- log10(c(10, 100, 1000, 10000))
by0 <- log10(30)                                  # multinomial: N_ch < 30 (binomial CLT, p-dependent)
# telegraph: N_ch * (Delta/tau) < 1  ->  ly < -lx  -> triangle in the bottom-left (clipped to the plot)
tri <- data.frame(x = c(-2, -2, -1), y = c(1, 2, 1))

g <- ggplot() +
  annotate("rect", xmin = -2, xmax = 0, ymin = 1, ymax = 4, fill = "#EAF3F7") +            # Gaussian base
  geom_polygon(data = tri, aes(x, y), fill = "#4393C3", alpha = .45) +                     # telegraph (N_ch.Δ<1)
  annotate("rect", xmin = -2, xmax = 0, ymin = 1, ymax = by0, fill = "#F4A582", alpha = .5) +  # multinomial (N_ch<30)
  annotate("segment", x = -2, xend = -1, y = 2, yend = 1,
           colour = "#21618c", linewidth = .5, linetype = "dashed") +                      # N_ch.Δ = 1
  annotate("segment", x = -1.7, xend = -0.2, y = 1.9, yend = 3.7,
           colour = "grey40", linewidth = .45, arrow = arrow(length = unit(.16, "cm"))) +
  annotate("text", x = -0.95, y = 2.75, angle = 32, size = 2.6, colour = "grey35",
           label = "increasing~Gaussianity~~(distortion %->% 1)", parse = TRUE) +
  annotate("text", x = -0.55, y = 3.62, size = 3.4, fontface = "bold", colour = "#1a5276",
           label = "Gaussian (CLT)") +
  annotate("text", x = -0.55, y = 3.40, size = 2.8, colour = "#1a5276",
           label = "distortion %~~% 1", parse = TRUE) +
  annotate("segment", x = -1.72, xend = -1.85, y = 2.32, yend = 1.55,
           colour = "#21618c", linewidth = .3) +                                   # leader to the triangle
  annotate("text", x = -1.62, y = 2.62, size = 3.0, fontface = "bold", colour = "#21618c",
           label = "Poisson /") +
  annotate("text", x = -1.62, y = 2.44, size = 3.0, fontface = "bold", colour = "#21618c",
           label = "telegraph") +
  annotate("text", x = -0.98, y = 1.62, size = 2.5, colour = "#21618c",
           label = "N[ch] * Delta < 1", parse = TRUE) +
  annotate("text", x = -0.6, y = 1.32, size = 3.2, fontface = "bold", colour = "#a04000",
           label = "multinomial") +
  annotate("text", x = -0.6, y = 1.15, size = 2.6, colour = "#a04000",
           label = "N[ch] < 30", parse = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = XB, labels = c(".01", ".1", "1"), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = YB, labels = c("10", "100", "1e3", "1e4"), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2, 0), ylim = c(1, 4)) +
  labs(x = expression(Delta / tau[min]), y = expression(N[ch])) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 9, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank(), axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 9))
g
ggsave("Figure_5_domains_schematic.pdf", g, width = 5.2, height = 4.2)
```
